channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: The water was their livelihood. Now Thailand's sea nomads work to preserve a vanishing way of life
item.description: SURIN ISLANDS, Thailand (AP) — When Hook was a child, he started his days by jumping off the boat that his family lived on and into the ocean. By age 3, he could already swim and dive in shallow waters. His home was a kabang, a boat, that his family sailed in Thailand’s southern waters.
item.dc:creator: HUIZHONG WU and JINTAMAS SAKSORNCHAI, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Top-ranking NYPD officer abruptly resigns amid sexual misconduct allegations
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The highest-ranking uniformed officer in the New York Police Department has resigned amid allegations he demanded sex from a subordinate in exchange for opportunities to earn extra pay.
item.dc:creator: PHILIP MARCELO, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Lara Trump says she will not seek to replace Rubio in Senate
item.description: The incoming president announced Rubio as his Secretary of State choice in November. Earlier this month, Lara Trump stepped down as RNC co-chair to “heavily consider” taking on the role in Congress's upper chamber.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KRON4

item.title: Driver killed by police after driving truck into Killeen Mall, at least 5 injured
item.description: A driver was shot and killed by law enforcement officers after driving a trucking into the Killeen Mall Saturday night, injuring at least five people, according to the Texas Department of Public Safety.
item.dc:creator: Cora Neas, NewsNation

item.title: What Trump's decision to wade into spending fight tells us about the next 4 years
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — After days of threats and demands, Donald Trump had little to show for it once lawmakers passed a budget deal in the early hours of Saturday, narrowly averting a pre-Christmas government shutdown.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, STEPHEN GROVES, JILL COLVIN and JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: UPDATE: Missing 63-year-old man from Pueblo located
item.description: UPDATE: SATURDAY 12/21/2024 6:38 p.m. (PUEBLO, Colo.) — PPD reported that Palacio has been located and thanks the community for their help. ORIGINAL HEADLINE: PPD requests help finding missing 63-year-old man SATURDAY 12/21/2024 4:53 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Dara Korn, NewsNation

item.title: Deal spares federal workers before Christmas but there's concern about possible job cuts under Trump
item.description: ANNAPOLIS, Md. (AP) — Johnny Zuagar said he tried to hide his worries about a potential government shutdown from his three boys as he weighed how much to spend on Christmas presents. “I’ve got to keep a poker face,” Zuagar, a statistician at the U.S.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN WITTE, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: What NYC Mayor Adams told Luigi Mangione when they met in NYC
item.description: New York City Mayor Eric Adams said he “wanted to send a strong message” when he met accused UnitedHealthcare CEO killer Luigi Mangione in New York City on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Finn Hoogensen, WGN 9

item.title: In a calendar rarity, Hanukkah starts this year on Christmas Day
item.description: Hanukkah, Judaism’s eight-day Festival of Lights, begins this year on Christmas Day, which has only happened four times since 1900. For some rabbis, the intersection of the two religious holidays provides an auspicious occasion for interfaith engagement.
item.dc:creator: DAVID CRARY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What to know about Hanukkah and how it's celebrated around the world
item.description: Hanukkah — also spelled Chanukah or other transliterations from Hebrew — is Judaism’s “festival of lights.” On eight consecutive nightfalls, Jews gather with family and friends to light one additional candle in the menorah — a multibranched candelabra.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: US flu season is underway, as cases surge in some areas and vaccinations lag
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The U.S. flu season is underway, with cases surging across much of the country, health officials said Friday. The Centers for Disease Control and Prevention noted sharp increases in several measures, including lab tests and emergency room visits.
item.dc:creator: MIKE STOBBE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 5 of the most frustrating health insurer tactics and why they exist
item.description: Even for those with health insurance, coverage does not ensure access to care, much less high-quality and affordable care.
item.dc:creator: Monica S. Aswani Assistant Professor of Health Services Administration, University of Alabama at Birmingham, KTLA 5

item.title: What to know about Hanukkah and how it's celebrated around the world
item.description: Hanukkah — also spelled Chanukah or other transliterations from Hebrew — is Judaism’s “festival of lights.”.
item.dc:creator: Giovanna Dell'Orto, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: UnitedHealthcare CEO murder suspect Luigi Mangione's looks captivate TikTok users after perp walk
item.description: TikTok is swooning over Luigi Mangione, the accused killer of UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson, after his perp walk in New York City on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Senate passes bill to boost Social Security benefits for some
item.description: The Senate voted overwhelmingly in the early hours of Saturday to pass legislation to boost Social Security benefits for more than 2 million Americans.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Senate sends spending deal to Biden's desk, averting shutdown
item.description: The Senate in the early hours of Saturday passed a stopgap funding package, avoiding a government shutdown that would have furloughed hundreds of thousands of federal workers and bringing a tumultuous week in Congress to a close.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Oregon sheriff concerned about letters asking people to track possible immigrants
item.description: LINCOLN CITY, Ore. (AP) — A sheriff in Oregon says he has contacted the FBI and is increasingly concerned about a letter circulating in his rural, coastal community asking people to write down the license plate numbers of possible immigrants.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: University of California campuses resolve discrimination complaints stemming from Gaza protests
item.description: The U.S. Department of Education said Friday it has reached an agreement with the University of California system resolving complaints from Jewish and Muslim students of discrimination and harassment during protests last spring over the war in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: JOCELYN GECKER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: At least 2 dead, 60 hurt after car drives into German Christmas market in a suspected attack
item.description: A car plowed into a busy outdoor Christmas market in the eastern German city of Magdeburg on Friday, killing at least two people and injuring at least 60 others in what authorities suspect was an attack.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: How a federal government shutdown could impact Californians
item.description: (KTLA) — The federal government could shut down at 12:01 a.m. Saturday unless Congress agrees on a new spending measure. For most Californians, the impact of a shutdown wouldn’t be felt immediately; services like Social Security, veterans, and unemployment benefits will still be distributed.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Tren de Aragua gang members arrested in NYC apartment next to daycare facility
item.description: Several suspected members of Tren De Aragua (TdA) were busted hiding out in a New York City apartment building located next to a daycare center, a law enforcement source tells Fox News.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hochul signs law to stop unauthorized restaurant reservations
item.description: Hochul signed a bill on requiring digital platforms to contract directly with restaurants in order to sell reservations there.
item.dc:creator: Johan Sheridan, NewsNation

item.title: Party City going out of business, starting process 'immediately': report
item.description: Party City, once among the top names in party-supply retailers, is going out of business, according to an announcement from CEO Barry Litwin that was viewed by CNN.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: US civil rights probe faults Philadelphia school district over handling of antisemitism reports
item.description: The Philadelphia school district failed to show it protected Jewish students from harassment despite “repeated, extensive notice” that students, teachers and administrators were engaging in antisemitic behavior, the U.S.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL RUBINKAM, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Closures, Social Security checks, furloughs: What a government shutdown might mean
item.description: Congress has until midnight Friday to come up with a way to fund the government or federal agencies will shut down, meaning hundreds of thousands of federal employees could be sent home — or stay on the job without pay — just ahead of the holidays.
item.dc:creator: MEG KINNARD, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Government shutdown is averted just after deadline as Congress rejects Trump's debt limit demands
item.description: The Senate has rushed through final passage of a bipartisan plan to fund federal operations and prevent a government shutdown.
item.dc:creator: Lisa Mascaro, Farnoush Amiri And Matt Brown, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Starbucks workers plan strikes that could hit hundreds of stores by Christmas Eve
item.description: Workers at Starbucks stores plan to go on a five-day strike starting Friday to protest lack of progress in contract negotiations with the company.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Man accused in UnitedHealthcare CEO killing faces federal charge that's eligible for death penalty
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The man accused of killing UnitedHealthcare’s CEO was whisked back to New York by plane and helicopter Thursday to face new federal charges of stalking and murder, which could bring the death penalty if he's convicted.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK, LARRY NEUMEISTER and MARK SCOLFORO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: UnitedHealthcare CEO murder 'acceptable' among 40% of young people
item.description: The poll was conducted the week after a man, suspected to be 26-year-old Luigi Mangione, allegedly shot and killed UnitedHealthcare CEO Bryan Thompson over his discontent with the American healthcare system.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, Fox 5

item.title: Former top aide to New York City's mayor pleads not guilty in bribery case
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The New York City mayor's former chief adviser, her son and two real estate investors were indicted Thursday on bribery and conspiracy charges that came as the mayor himself fights a separate corruption case.
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ and JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Taco Bell adding chicken nuggets to menus nationwide
item.description: Taco Bell, known for its Mexican-inspired cuisine, will begin serving its take on chicken nuggets later this month. Taco Bell’s Crispy Chicken Nuggets features tortilla breading and will be served with three sauces, including the first-ever Hidden Valley Fire Ranch.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, WGN 9
